<!-- Generated by agents-reverse-engineer v1.2.17 -->

# attractor-dot

Parses strict Graphviz DOT dialect for Attractor pipeline definitions. Converts `digraph` source into typed AST via winnow combinators. Handles node/edge/subgraph declarations, attribute blocks, C++-style comments, and edge chain expansion. Consumed by `attractor-pipeline` for graph execution.

## Contents

- **[src/ast.rs](./src/ast.rs)** ‚Äî `DotGraph`, `NodeDef`, `EdgeDef`, `SubgraphDef`, `AttributeValue` enum with `Duration` variant using custom serde.
- **[src/duration_serde.rs](./src/duration_serde.rs)** ‚Äî Serde adapters for `std::time::Duration`. Serializes to `"{ms}ms"`, parses suffixes `ms/s/m/h/d` with multipliers 1/1/60/3600/86400.
- **[src/lib.rs](./src/lib.rs)** ‚Äî Crate entry. Exports `ast`, `duration_serde`, `parse` function returning `Result<DotGraph, AttractorError>`. Documents quoted duration string behavior.
- **[src/parser.rs](./src/parser.rs)** ‚Äî Winnow-based parser. `parse` strips comments, invokes `parse_digraph`, maps offsets to `(line, col)`. `merge_statements` expands edge chains and applies default attribute inheritance.
- **[benches/parse.rs](./benches/parse.rs)** ‚Äî Criterion benchmarks targeting `attractor_dot::parse`. Generates synthetic DOT fixtures programmatically.

## Stack

- Rust workspace member
- `winnow` 0.7 for parser combinators
- `serde` for attribute serialization
- `criterion` dev dependency for benchmarks
- Depends on workspace crate `attractor-types` for `AttractorError`

## Behavioral Contracts

**Duration Formats**

- Serialization template: `{}ms` (milliseconds only)
- Parsing suffixes: `ms` (√ó1), `s` (√ó1), `m` (√ó60), `h` (√ó3600), `d` (√ó86400)

**Type Parsing Patterns**

- Identifier: `[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*`
- Qualified ID: `identifier ( '.' identifier )+`
- Float: `(opt(alt(('-', '+'))), digit1, '.', digit1)`
- Integer: `(opt(alt(('-', '+'))), digit1)`

**Quoted Duration Handling**
Quoted strings matching duration syntax (e.g., `"20m"`, `"500ms"`) parse as `AttributeValue::String`, not `AttributeValue::Duration`. Downstream must explicitly invoke `parse_duration_str`.

**Error Messages**

- `strict` prefix rejected: `'digraph' keyword (strict graphs are not supported)`
- Undirected `graph` rejected: `'digraph' keyword (undirected graphs are not supported)`
- `--` operator rejected: `only directed edges (->); undirected edges (--) are not supported`

**Comment Processing**
`strip_comments` removes `//` line and `/* */` block comments while preserving newlines to maintain line number alignment for `offset_to_line_col` error reporting.

**Edge Chain Expansion**
Syntax `A -> B -> C` expands to pairwise `EdgeDef` entries `(A,B)` and `(B,C)` in `merge_statements`, sharing the statement's attribute set.

**Attribute Default Inheritance**
`node [attr=val]` and `edge [attr=val]` statements apply to subsequent node/edge definitions within scope; inherited by subgraphs via `parent_node_defaults`/`parent_edge_defaults` parameters in `merge_statements`.

**Benchmark Fixture Templates**

- Node declaration: `node{} [shape="box",label="Node {}",max_retries="3",timeout="30s"]`
- Edge declaration: `node{} -> node{};`
- Subgraph cluster: `subgraph cluster_{} { inner{}_a -> inner{}_b; }`

## API Surface

- `parse(input: &str) -> Result<DotGraph, AttractorError>` ‚Äî Entry point exposed in `lib.rs` and implemented in `parser.rs`
- `ast` module ‚Äî Exports `DotGraph`, `NodeDef`, `EdgeDef`, `SubgraphDef`, `AttributeValue`
- `duration_serde` module ‚Äî Exports `serialize`, `deserialize`, `parse_duration_str(s: &str) -> Result<Duration, AttractorError>`
- `AttractorError::ParseError { line, col, ... }` ‚Äî 1-indexed position reporting on syntax failures

## Architecture

Input pipeline: `parse` ‚Üí `strip_comments` (newline-preserving) ‚Üí `parse_digraph` (winnow combinators for identifiers, strings, values, statements) ‚Üí `merge_statements` (edge chain expansion, default inheritance, implicit node creation) ‚Üí `DotGraph` AST.

Error handling: `offset_to_line_col(original, remaining_len, stripped_len)` computes 1-indexed `(line, col)` by comparing original source against stripped text (newline-preserved).

## File Relationships

- `parser.rs` imports AST types via `crate::ast::*` and constructs `DotGraph` instances
- `ast.rs` references `crate::duration_serde` for `AttributeValue::Duration` variant
- `lib.rs` re-exports `ast` and `duration_serde` modules, exposing `parse` as public interface
- `AttractorError` type imported from workspace crate `attractor-types`
- Benchmarks in `benches/parse.rs` target `attractor_dot::parse` interface; no external file dependencies
